page: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Esv6 family, virtualized via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores running at a base frequency of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. There are no GPUs or local storage drives included, though the server is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance, with read-only and write-only large block memory bandwidth placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Computational benchmarks show strong multi-core and single-core Geekbench scores, alongside top-tier performance in image processing. Database operations and Redis SET operations both achieved results in the top 25%, with Redis handling 66.87 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's AMX instructions support strong prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large language models, though text generation speeds for larger models fall into the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E96-48s_v6 offers high memory density per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads without requiring the purchase of excess compute cores. The primary hardware tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, requiring reliance on remote Premium Storage options which may affect I/O latency. This server is configured for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Esv6 family, powered by a 24-core Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 48 dedicated vCPUs. It features 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a high ratio of 16.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities, with memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis SET operations scoring in the top 25%. The processor's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) enable strong prompt processing speeds for language models. Lacking local storage, the instance relies on remote Premium Storage. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale data compilation, and CPU-based inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v6 is a memory-optimized server with 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v6 is an Intel-based memory-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and strong database benchmark performance.
tagline: A memory-dense, Intel-powered virtual server featuring high memory bandwidth and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8573C processors at 4.2 GHz.
768.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per core memory density.
No local storage included, requiring remote Premium Storage configurations.
Strong memory bandwidth and database performance in the top 25% of benchmarks.
Intel AMX support delivering strong prompt processing speeds for LLM inference.
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and large-scale data compilation.
